findings.title: CPU & Memory metrics are simulated using Math.random()
findings.description: CPU and Memory values fluctuate randomly every 3 seconds. No actual system monitoring.
findings.recommendation: Implement psutil.cpu_percent() and psutil.virtual_memory() on backend

findings.title: All API latency values are hardcoded
findings.description: Latency chart shows static values (45ms, 98ms, 120ms, etc.) that never change
findings.impact: Cannot detect performance degradation or optimization opportunities
findings.recommendation: Implement request timing middleware to measure actual latency

findings.title: Live trace logs are randomly generated fiction
findings.description: Logs are created using Math.random() every 3 seconds with pre-defined messages
findings.impact: Debugging is impossible, cannot trace actual system events
findings.recommendation: Stream real logs from backend using structured logging

findings.title: Cache Hit Ratio metric is completely fake
findings.description: Shows 94.2% cache hit rate, but no caching layer exists in the system
findings.recommendation: Either remove metric or implement actual caching (Redis/in-memory)

findings.title: Database pool metrics misleading for SQLite
findings.description: Shows connection pool (2/10), but SQLite doesn't use connection pools this way
findings.recommendation: Replace with actual SQLite metrics (query latency, transaction rate)

findings.title: Health check statuses are hardcoded
findings.impact: Cannot detect service failures or degradation
findings.recommendation: Implement actual health check endpoints for each service

findings.title: WebSocket connection count is static
findings.description: Always shows '1 active', doesn't reflect actual connections

findings.title: No backend observability endpoints exist
findings.description: Frontend has no way to get real telemetry data from backend
findings.impact: Entire observability dashboard is a facade
